expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Test Pyramid for This Service' table with Unit ~65%, Integration ~25%, Contract, E2E ~10% layers and what each covers.

Dedicated 'Mocking Strategy' section with MSW network-level mocking for Stripe API. Includes concrete MSW handler code for stripe.com/v1/payment_intents endpoints. States: 'Mock at the boundary, not inside your code.'

Phase 3 is entirely dedicated to webhook handlers with integration tests covering signature verification (valid, invalid, missing, expired), payment_intent events (succeeded, failed, unknown), and webhook-level idempotency (duplicate event deduplication).

Phase 1 is dedicated to idempotency logic: state transitions (pending->completed, pending->failed, completed->no transition), key validation with boundary values, request deduplication decision table. E2E section also includes a double-charge prevention test.

Full directory tree under 'Test Organization' section showing src/ co-located unit tests, tests/integration/, tests/contracts/, tests/e2e/, tests/helpers/factories/, tests/mocks/, and schemas/ directories.

expectations.text: Prioritizes which tests to write first
expectations.evidence: Explicit phased priority order: Phase 1 Idempotency Logic (highest risk), Phase 2 Retry Mechanism, Phase 3 Webhook Handlers, Phase 4 Payment Calculations, Phase 5 Contract Tests, Phase 6 E2E. States 'critical paths (auth, payments, data mutations) need 100% coverage' to justify ordering.
